title: Regan Grace

[[Calvin Wellington]] (cousin) }} }} '''Regan Grace''' (born 12 December 1996) is a Welsh professional dual-code rugby footballer who plays [[rugby union]] as a wing for [[Bath Rugby|Bath]]. He started his professional career coming through the academy and playing six seasons with English [[rugby league|league]] side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12206/9954643/three-more-youngsters-agree-new-deals-with-st-helens|title=Three more youngsters agree new deals with St Helens|publisher=Sky Sports|date=18 August 2015|access-date=10 September 2016}} He has represented [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at international level for rugby league and rugby union.{{cite web|url=http://www.walesonline.co.uk/sport/other-sport/rugby-league/wales-rugby-league-stars-braced-10355358|title=Wales rugby league stars braced for French European challenge in Cardiff|publisher=Wales Online|date=29 October 2015|access-date=10 September 2016}} ==Early life== Grace was born in [[Port Talbot]], Wales and is of [[Jamaicans|Jamaican]] descent. He was educated at St Joseph's Catholic School in Port Talbot.https://www.wru.wales/player/regan-grace/ He is the brother of television personality [[Lateysha Grace]], and cousin of rugby footballer [[Calvin Wellington]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41985502|title = Light in the World Cup gloom for Kear|work = BBC Sport |date=14 November 2017}} Grace originally played [[rugby union]], and started his career at [[Aberavon Quins RFC]].https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/2397/black-history-month---regan-graces-story He was also a member of the [[Ospreys (rugby union)|Ospreys]] youth teams. ==Rugby league career== === St Helens === Grace joined St Helens in 2014, when he was only 17. Despite not making his first-team debut for St Helens until 2017, he was first picked to play for the Welsh international side in October 2015 when he came on as an interchange player in Wales' 14–6 victory over {{rlnt|France}}.{{cite news |last1=Heppenstall |first1=Ross |title=Amazing Grace - Wales star Regan happy to put rugby league in the spotlight |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wales-st-helens-regan-grace-19939132 |access-date=11 February 2022 |work=Manchester Evening News |date=1 March 2021}} His first Super League appearance came on Good Friday, 14 April, 2017 in the derby match against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] where he scored a try on debut.{{cite news |last1=Williams |first1=David |title=How Port Talbot youngster has followed well-trodden path to rugby league success |url=https://www.thenational.wales/news/19272562.amazing-grace-thrives-saints-go-marching/ |access-date=11 February 2022 |work=The National Wales |date=1 May 2021 |language=en}} The following month he signed a new two-and-a-half-year contract with St Helens.{{cite news|title=Regan Grace: St Helens and Wales winger signs new two-and-a-half year contract|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40093050|work=BBC Sport|date=30 May 2017}} Since that moment, Grace was a regular in the St Helens side making 139 appearances by the end of the 2021 season and scoring 87 tries. Among those matches have been two [[Challenge Cup]] finals. The first was the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}} The second was the 26–12 [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}} During the 2021 Challenge Cup Grace was the leading try-scorer, scoring seven including a [[hat-trick]] against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the quarter-finals, and two in the semi-final against [[Hull FC]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/watch/video/12300066/grace-hat-trick-hero-in-qfs/|title=Grace hat-trick hero in QFs|website=www.skysports.com}}{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}{{cite book |editor1-last=Butcher |editor1-first=Tim |editor2-last=Spencer |editor2-first=Daniel |title=Rugby League Yearbook 2021-2022 |date=2021 |isbn=978-190134740-1 |page=303|publisher=LEAGUE PUBLICATIONS Limited }} Grace has also played in three Super League Grand Finals as St Helens won the [[2019 Super League Grand Final|2019]], [[2020 Super League Grand Final|2020]] and [[2021 Super League Grand Final|2021]] Grand Finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport}} On 5 July 2022, Grace signed a contract to join [[Rugby Union]] side [[Racing 92]] in 2023.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62049019|title=Regan Grace: St Helens winger to switch codes with Racing 92 after 2022 season|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 2 August 2022, it was announced that Grace had ruptured his achilles during St Helens heavy defeat against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] which would rule him out for the remainder of the 2022 Super League season.{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/20596654.st-helens-wing-regan-grace-ruled-ruptured-achilles/|title=St Helens wing Regan Grace ruled out for season with ruptured Achilles|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}} === Wales === He played for Wales in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe|2017 World Cup qualifiers]], scoring two tries in the country's first game. He was selected in the Wales 9s squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].{{cite web |date=9 October 2019 |title=16-man squad for 9s World Cup revealed |url=https://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16722/-man-squad-for-s-world-cup-revealed |access-date=31 October 2019 |publisher=Wales RL}} ==Rugby union career== === Racing 92 === Grace signed for [[Top 14]] side [[Racing 92]] in 2022, but was ruled out with a significant achilles injury.{{Cite web |title=The Regan Grace interview: My new target and long-term Wales hope after World Cup dream was wrecked - Wales Online |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-news/regan-grace-interview-new-target-26796554 |access-date=2024-06-22 |website=www.walesonline.co.uk}} While recovering, Grace suffered a recurrence of the same achilles injury. === Bath === Grace was released by Racing 92, and in November 2023 linked up with [[Premiership Rugby|Premiership]] team [[Bath Rugby|Bath]] to continue his rehab.{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=2023-11-29 |title=Ex-St Helens flyer Regan Grace doing rehab with new club after exit from rugby union side Racing 92 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-st-helens-flyer-regan-grace-doing-rehab-with-new-club-after-exit-from-rugby-union-side-racing-92/ |access-date=2024-06-22 |website=Total Rugby League |language=en-US}} Grace signed a short-term contract with Bath on 16 February 2024, and made his debut on 9 March 2024, in a friendly against [[Gloucester Rugby|Gloucester]].{{Cite news |date=2024-02-16 |title=Regan Grace: Former St Helens winger joins Bath Rugby from Racing 92 |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/68321641 |access-date=2024-06-22 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}{{Cite web |last1=Jess |first1=Sam |last2=Evely |first2=John |date=2024-03-11 |title=Johann van Graan gives his verdict on Regan Grace's Bath Rugby debut |url=https://www.somersetlive.co.uk/sport/other-sport/he-something-special-inside-him-9156866 |access-date=2024-06-22 |website=Somerset Live |language=en}} On 24 April 2024, Grace signed with Bath for the [[2024–25 Premiership Rugby|2024–25 Premiership]] season.{{Cite web |date=2024-04-24 |title=Regan Grace: Bath sign ex-St Helens rugby league wing for 2024-25 |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/articles/c1wx0epnjdxo |access-date=2024-06-22 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}} Grace made his Bath debut on 23 November 2024 in a Premiership Cup fixture against [[Bedford Blues]].{{Cite web |title=Bath Rugby debut for Grace {{!}} Bath Rugby |url=https://www.bathrugby.com/content/bath-rugby-debut-for-grace |access-date=2025-01-06 |website=www.bathrugby.com |language=en}} He made his European debut against [[Benetton Rugby|Benetton]] on 15 December 2024.{{Cite web |last=James |first=Ben |date=2025-01-04 |title=Regan Grace targeted by Welsh club as solution to injury crisis |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-news/regan-grace-targeted-welsh-club-30708453 |access-date=2025-01-06 |website=Wales Online |language=en}} === Cardiff === On 6 January 2025, Grace joined [[Cardiff Rugby|Cardiff]] on a six-month contract.{{Cite web |date=2025-01-06 |title=Regan Grace: Cardiff sign Bath's ex-rugby league winger |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/articles/clykdj2l979o |access-date=2025-01-06 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}} He made his debut on 10 January 2025 in the Challenge Cup against [[USA Perpignan]].{{Cite web |date=2025-01-10 |title=Perpignan 23-20 Cardiff: Regan Grace injured as visitors edged out |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/articles/cp8qnqy521eo |access-date=2025-05-22 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}} Grace was released by Cardiff at the end of the season.{{Cite web |last=Jones |first=John |date=2025-05-22 |title=Nine players leave Cardiff as legend departs and Wales star left without club |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-news/nine-players-leave-cardiff-legend-31697048 |access-date=2025-05-22 |website=Wales Online |language=en}} ===Sale=== On 21 November 2025 it was reported that he would turn out for a trial match for [[Sale Sharks]] v [[Bath Rugby|Bath]].{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=21 Nov 2025|accessdate=22 Nov 2025|title=Wales dual-code wing Grace to play on trial for Sale|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/articles/cwyx5n93ygro}} On 27 November 2025, he had signed a short term contract with [[Sale Sharks]]https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/articles/c36zer83e63o{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Regan Grace agrees contract with new club as Super League interest revealed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/regan-grace-agrees-contract-with-new-club-as-super-league-interest-revealed}} === Wales === He was called up to the [[Wales national rugby union team]] for the first time on 20 June, after [[Keelan Giles]] was ruled out of the [[2024 Wales rugby union tour of Australia|summer tour of Australia]], playing in the uncapped match against Queensland Reds, scoring a try on debut.{{cite news |title=Shock Wales call-up for uncapped wing Regan Grace |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/articles/cmjjmdk5pmko |website=BBC Sport |date=20 June 2024 |accessdate=20 June 2024 }}{{Cite web |title=Queensland Reds v Wales - live text updates from Brisbane |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/live/cv2g161l9p0t |access-date=2025-01-07 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}} ==References== ;General *[https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2025/apr/04/regan-grace-rugby-codes-clubs-st-helens-cardiff Regan Grace’s long road back to rugby: two codes, four clubs and 979 days] ;Inline
